message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This study is the first of its kind to assess the impact of preemptive therapeutic dose anticoagulation on mortality compared to prophylactic anticoagulation among COVID-19 patients. Its findings provide insight to clinicians regarding the management of COVID-19, particularly with the known prothrombotic state.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Research Question</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    To determine the impact of anticoagulation on in-hospital mortality among COVID-19 positive patients with the
                    <jats:italic>a priori</jats:italic>
                    hypothesis that there would be a lower risk of in-hospital mortality with use of preemptive therapeutic over prophylactic dose enoxaparin or heparin.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Study Design and Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Study Design</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Retrospective cohort study from April 1 - April 25, 2020. The date of final follow-up was June 12, 2020.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Setting</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Two large, acute care hospitals in Western Connecticut.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Participant</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Five hundred and one inpatients were identified after discharge as 18 years or older and positive for SARS-CoV-2. The final sample size included 374 patients after applying exclusion criteria. Demographic variables were collected via hospital billing inquiries, while the clinical variables were abstracted from patients’ medical records.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Exposure</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Preemptive enoxaparin or heparin at a therapeutic or prophylactic dose.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Main Outcom</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>In-hospital mortality.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>When comparing preemptive therapeutic to prophylactic anticoagulation through multi-variable analysis, risk of in-hospital mortality was 2.3 times greater in patients receiving preemptive therapeutic anticoagulation (95% CI = 1.0, 4.9; p = 0.04).</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Interpretation</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>An increase in in-hospital mortality was observed with preemptive therapeutic anticoagulation. Thus, in the management of COVID-19 and its complications, we recommend further research and cautious use of preemptive therapeutic over prophylactic anticoagulation.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                </jats:sec>
title: Clinical Outcomes With the Use of Prophylactic Versus Therapeutic Anticoagulation in COVID-19

reference.unstructured: WHO Director-General’s remarks at the media briefing on 2019-nCoV on 11 February 2020. Accessed April 11, 2020. https://www.who.int/dg/speeches/detail/who-director-general-s-remarks-at-the-media-briefing-on-2019-ncov-on-11-february-2020.
WHO Coronavirus Disease (COVID-19) Dashboard. Accessed May 10, 2020. https://covid19.who.int/.

F.A. Klok , M.J.H.A. Kruip , N.J.M. van der Meer , M.S. Arbous , D.A.M.P.J. Gommers , K.M. Kant , F.H.J. Kaptein , J. van Paassen , M.A.M. Stals , M.V. Huisman , H. Endeman . Incidence of thrombotic complications in critically ill ICU patients with COVID-19 Thromb. Res. (2020), doi:https://doi.org/10.1016/j.thromres.2020.04.041
